task_completion.description: Microsoft Edge is open and DevTools is used to inspect the Application/Storage tab for the target websites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Edge window is visible. DevTools is open with the 'Application' tab selected, showing the Storage pane (Local Storage, Session Storage, IndexedDB, Cookies) for Wikipedia, GitHub, or Reddit.

task_completion.description: The storage audit report file is created at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file `/home/ga/Desktop/storage_audit_report.txt`, or the file icon is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The report contains a section for Wikipedia with specific storage key names and counts for all four required categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text in the report clearly mentions 'Wikipedia', lists specific names of cookies or local storage keys (e.g., WMF-Last-Access, GeoIP), and provides approximate counts for Cookies, Local Storage, Session Storage, and IndexedDB.

task_completion.description: The report contains a section for GitHub with specific storage key names and counts for all four required categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text in the report clearly mentions 'GitHub', lists specific names of cookies or local storage keys (e.g., _gh_sess, logged_in), and provides approximate counts for Cookies, Local Storage, Session Storage, and IndexedDB.

task_completion.description: The report contains a section for Reddit with specific storage key names and counts for all four required categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text in the report clearly mentions 'Reddit', lists specific names of cookies or local storage keys (e.g., csv, edgebucket), and provides approximate counts for Cookies, Local Storage, Session Storage, and IndexedDB.

task_completion.description: The report includes a summary identifying which site stores the most data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A concluding sentence or section in the report explicitly states which of the three sites (Wikipedia, GitHub, or Reddit) stores the most data overall.

integrity.description: The agent used Microsoft Edge to perform the audit.
integrity.visual_evidence: Microsoft Edge browser UI is visible in the screenshots, not just a terminal window running curl or python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actually inspected the storage using DevTools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Edge DevTools 'Application' tab open, displaying actual storage data for the target sites.

integrity.description: The report contains genuine, site-specific storage data rather than fabricated or generic placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The cookie and storage key names in the report match real-world data for these sites (e.g., WMF-Last-Access, _gh_sess, edgebucket) and are not just generic terms like 'cookie1', 'test_key'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target domains are wikipedia.org, github.com, and reddit.com. The report must be at /home/ga/Desktop/storage_audit_report.txt. Expected cookies/keys to look for as proof of genuine inspection: Wikipedia (WMF-Last-Access, GeoIP, centralauth), GitHub (_gh_sess, logged_in, color_mode), Reddit (csv, edgebucket, loid). The report must explicitly mention counts for four categories: Cookies, Local Storage, Session Storage, and IndexedDB for each site.
